title: antirrhinum muralis
intro: Antirrhinum muralis, also known as the Downy Toadflax, is a perennial plant in the figwort family native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Antirrhinum muralis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Antirrhinum muralis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion to utilize its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
